commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide whether the partial text-only recovery scope is acceptable and require real restart-recovery proof before merge; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff adds no dependencies, scripts, permissions, or secret handling, and recovered user text is wrapped as untrusted prompt data. (sha=494575e9a619934b1a7493d6951cf35994b35126)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes main-session restart recovery to re-present the last user message for inspectable text-only assistant tails, adds content-block text extraction support, and expands regression coverage around restart recovery tails.

PR surface: Source +223, Tests +489. Total +712 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.6.10 still send the unresumable notice and mark the session failed when the tail is not resumable. I did not run a live gateway restart reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide whether the partial text-only recovery scope is acceptable and require real restart-recovery proof before merge; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: [AI] fix(agents): recover from non-resumable assistant tail by re-presenting last user message
target.body:
## Summary

Gateway restart recovery for non-resumable assistant tails now re-presents the last user message from the transcript with an idempotency guard. All recovery functions handle both raw API and OpenClaw canonical content-block formats. Recovered text is wrapped as untrusted prompt data; no display-level sanitizer is applied to recovered user content.

- Problem: Sessions with assistant-tail interruptions failed silently after gateway restart, forcing user retypes. Recovery only handled raw API formats and the physical last message.
- Solution: Detect pending tool calls in canonical formats (camelCase + snake_case). Extract user text from string and content-block arrays. Use meaningful transcript tail. Expand lookup window for long tool-loop transcripts. Wrap recovered text via `wrapUntrustedPromptDataBlock` (input-safe sanitizer, not agent-output sanitizer).
- What changed: `main-session-restart-recovery.ts` (+238): `hasAssistantToolCalls()`, `findLastUserMessage()`, `resolveMainSessionResumeBlockReason()`, `buildResumeMessage()` idempotency guard + untrusted wrapper via `wrapUntrustedPromptDataBlock` with `maxChars`, `hasInspectableContent()` guard. `main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts` (+526): 53 tests including delimiter-shaped + sentinel-shaped content regression. `content-blocks.ts` (+8): `input_text`/`output_text` types.
- What did NOT change: Resumable tail paths, approval-pending tail recovery (still fail-closed), auto-resume session detection.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #95609
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

When the gateway restarts and the transcript tail is a non-resumable assistant message, the current behavior silently fails the session and forces the user to retype from scratch. This PR recovers from that state by re-presenting the last user message with an idempotency guard. Recovered text is wrapped as untrusted prompt data using the input-safe `sanitizeForPromptLiteral` (inside `wrapUntrustedPromptDataBlock`), not the agent-output display sanitizer.

**Scope**: Text-only assistant tails only. Tool-call and approval-pending tails remain fail-closed per #95609 scope.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Non-resumable assistant-tail recovery with content-block format support, meaningful tail selection, untrusted prompt-data wrapper, and sentinel-shaped content preservation
- Real environment tested: Linux 4.19, Node 22.19, branch `fix/restart-recovery-user-retype-95609` @ `aa50ce80`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/lint-suppressions.test.ts --run
```

- Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts --run
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  53 passed (53)
   Start at  12:03:24
   Duration  5.63s

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/lint-suppressions.test.ts --run
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.root.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)

HEAD commit: aa50ce80b1
```

- Observed result after fix:

| # | Behavior | Result |
|---|----------|--------|
| 1 | Detects pending tool calls in 6 canonical formats | ✅ `toolCall`, `toolUse`, `functionCall`, `tool_use`, `tool_call`, `function_call` |
| 2 | Extracts user text from string and content-block arrays | ✅ both string and `content: [{type: "text", text: "..."}]` |
| 3 | Uses meaningful transcript tail | ✅ skips trailing system/compaction messages |
| 4 | Expands lookup beyond 20-message window | ✅ 200-message / 1 MB fallback |
| 5 | Injects idempotency guard | ✅ "review the transcript to determine which tool calls were already completed" |
| 6 | Fail-closed on uninspectable tails | ✅ empty/missing/truncated/oversized → fallback to failure |
| 7 | Fail-closed on stale approval-pending | ✅ still sends resend notice (no auto-recovery) |
| 8 | Wraps recovered text as untrusted prompt data | ✅ input-safe `sanitizeForPromptLiteral` inside wrapper (not agent-output sanitizer) |
| 9 | Regression: delimiter-shaped content injection | ✅ injection-shaped text inside `<untrusted-text>` tags |
| 10 | Regression: sentinel-marker-shaped content | ✅ `<<<BEGIN_UNTRUSTED_CHILD_RESULT>>>` preserved (HTML-escaped) inside wrapper |
| 11 | Recovery success (unit test) | ✅ `recovered=1 failed=0` |

- What was not tested: Full gateway agent resume with paired device credentials; live gateway restart-recovery roundtrip on a systemd host

## Root Cause

N/A — this is a feature enhancement. Prior behavior was intentional but incomplete. Content-block format gaps existed because the original implementation only handled raw API message shapes and the physical last message.

## Regression Test Plan

Target file: `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`. 53 tests covering: all 6 canonical content-block tool call formats, content-block user message, trailing system/compaction, 200-msg fallback, raw `tool_calls[]` detection, text-only assistant tail, empty/missing content, placeholder strings, `input_text` extraction, no-user-message, approval-pending, `__openclaw.truncated` metadata, delimiter-shaped content, sentinel-marker-shaped content.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users whose sessions were interrupted at assistant-tail boundaries will now see their last request re-presented in the transcript with an automatic continue prompt. Tool-call tails and approval-pending tails remain fail-closed per #95609.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No
- [x] Security hardening: recovered user text wrapped as untrusted prompt data; input-safe `sanitizeForPromptLiteral` used (not agent-output display sanitizer); sentinel-marker-shaped content preserved

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: All 6 canonical content-block tool call formats; content-block user messages; trailing system/compaction; 200-msg fallback window; `__openclaw.truncated` metadata; approval-pending fail-closed; untrusted prompt-data wrapper with delimiter-shaped + sentinel-shaped content regression tests
- Edge cases checked: mixed content blocks, empty content array, user-role message, system-sentinel-shaped user text
- What you did NOT verify: Full gateway resume with paired credentials

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes — all fixes reuse existing canonical helpers. The untrusted prompt-data wrapper reuses the established `wrapUntrustedPromptDataBlock` pattern. The display sanitizer is intentionally NOT applied to recovered user text (it was designed for agent output, not user input).
- **Refactor needed**: No
- **Alternative considered**: Using `sanitizePendingFinalDeliveryText` on recovered user text — rejected per ClawSweeper review (agent-output display sanitizer can alter/erase user content). The wrapper's internal `sanitizeForPromptLiteral` is the correct input-safe choice.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-flash
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest-risk area**: The idempotency guard is a prompt-level instruction, not a runtime enforcement gate. Scope is limited to text-only assistant tails — tool-call and approval-pending tails remain fail-closed.
- **Mitigation**: Explicit instruction placed at start of recovery message. For assistant-tail recovery, risk is lower than tool-result tails (no completed tool outputs to replay). Recovered text uses input-safe sanitizer, not display sanitizer. Sentinel-shaped user content is preserved (HTML-escaped) inside the untrusted prompt-data block.
- **Compatibility impact**: None.

---

Related to #95609
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan
